response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, no DNN training/inference optimization, no SoC modeling, and no relevant PhD/Master's credential in a hardware-adjacent field. Background is almost entirely application-layer software, which is a fundamental mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI hardware.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) of professional software engineering across diverse stacks
Some low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic with DLang and async I/O
Demonstrated knowledge sharing through workshops, talks, and open-source contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator workload analysis background
Educational path (BS only) does not meet the hardware-specialized Principal-level bar

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No GPU / SIMD / MIMD or DNN optimization background
Application-layer software career instead of hardware/perf architecture
